label: Undertext text unit label, e.g. name of the undertext
summary: A summary of the undertext content. Describes what has been identified in the undertext layer, typically recovered via multispectral imaging.
locus: Locus of the undertext, e.g. ff. 10-25, 30-45

work_wit.locus: Locus of the undertext work witness
work_wit.as_written: Undertext incipit as written in the original script

excerpt.as_written: First preserved line of the undertext as written
translation: English translation of the first preserved line
note: Text begins imperfectly; opening section lost

excerpt.as_written: Last preserved line of the undertext as written
translation: English translation of the last preserved line
note: Text ends imperfectly; remainder lost or illegible
A note about the undertext work witness, e.g. text identification ongoing.
A second note about the undertext work witness.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation for the undertext identification.

para.locus: Locus of the rubric in the undertext
para.as_written: Rubric text as written in the undertext
note: Rubric partially legible under multispectral imaging

note.value: This is a note about the undertext text unit. Undertext records often note the method of recovery and the degree of legibility.
This is a condition note for the undertext, e.g. heavily abraded; text recoverable only under multispectral imaging.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation for a reference work about the undertext.
note: A note about this bibliographic entry

program.label: Description program name, e.g. Sinai Arabic Manuscripts Project
program.description: Full description of the program under which this record was created, including dates, funders, and project goals. This can be a long paragraph.

desc_provenance.rights: Rights statement for the descriptive metadata.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or migration from legacy system.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent update.
